evals.prompt: What do I have on my calendar today?
evals.expected_output: A chronological Asia/Singapore agenda using native Calendar data, excluding cancelled events and clear working-location markers, with real event links when available.
assertions: The search sub-command is selected
The query covers local midnight through the next local midnight
Working-location markers are excluded without hiding real meetings
No Calendar data is invented

evals.prompt: Find meetings with Mani in the next 30 days.
evals.expected_output: A deduplicated chronological list filtered by attendee evidence, with pagination or date-range chunking if the response is too large.
assertions: Attendee matching is conservative and email evidence is preferred
Large results use pagination, narrower queries, or weekly/daily chunks
At most 50 unique events are shown unless the user asks for more
Truncation is disclosed

evals.prompt: Find standup meetings in the next 30 days.
evals.expected_output: A title-filtered event list that uses server-side text search when supported and deduplicates recurring instances correctly.
assertions: Server-side title or text filtering is preferred
Recurring instances are deduplicated by event ID plus start time
Results are sorted chronologically

evals.prompt: Show my meetings next Tuesday and include whether I accepted them.
evals.expected_output: A date-scoped agenda containing a top-level My RSVP field for each event.
assertions: The relative date is resolved from the host's current date
my_response_status is derived from Calendar data
Declined events may be displayed but are not treated as busy

evals.prompt: Show me all my meetings this week.
evals.expected_output: A Monday-to-Friday chronological agenda with coverage and truncation disclosed.
assertions: A work-week request uses Monday through Friday
All-day working-location markers are filtered
Partial or truncated coverage is stated

evals.prompt: Am I available this Saturday at 10 AM?
evals.expected_output: A NO result explaining the weekday rule without calling Calendar because the static rule decides the answer.
assertions: The available sub-command is selected
Weekend availability is rejected
Calendar is not called unnecessarily

evals.prompt: Can I take a one-hour meeting tomorrow at 1 PM?
evals.expected_output: A NO result explaining that the request overlaps the 12 PM to 2 PM lunch window.
assertions: The full requested duration is evaluated
Any lunch overlap makes the slot unavailable
Calendar is not called when lunch alone decides the answer

evals.prompt: Check whether tomorrow at 3 PM is free for one hour. Assume a confirmed event ends at 2:45 PM.
evals.expected_output: A NO result because the requested start leaves only a 15-minute gap after the prior event.
assertions: A 30-minute buffer is enforced before the requested meeting
The conflicting event and timing are reported
No event is created or changed

evals.prompt: Can I have an in-person meeting at Marina Bay tomorrow at 3 PM? Assume another confirmed physical meeting already exists that day.
evals.expected_output: A NO result applying the one-physical-meeting-per-day rule.
assertions: The requested meeting is classified as physical from credible venue evidence
Existing events are classified using all physical-meeting conditions
The second physical meeting is declined

evals.prompt: Can I meet in person tomorrow at 3 PM? The only existing event location is 'Office' and it has no other location details.
evals.expected_output: A clarification asking whether the ambiguous existing event is physical before applying the one-physical-meeting rule.
assertions: A generic location is treated as ambiguous
The skill does not automatically count the event as physical
The user is asked to confirm only because classification changes the result

evals.prompt: Does this meeting fit my typical profile: 60 minutes, online, with a Google Meet link?
evals.expected_output: Only the word yes, without accessing Calendar.
assertions: The meeting sub-command is selected
All three profile conditions are evaluated
The output is only yes
No Calendar connector is used

evals.prompt: Am I free tomorrow at 3 PM? Assume no Calendar capability is available.
evals.expected_output: A clear statement that availability cannot be verified, followed by a stop with no guess or event change.
assertions: The missing Calendar capability is reported
Availability is not inferred from other sources
No non-native connector is used without explicit approval
No event is created or changed
